transcript.text: I did it my way and I did it on my own volition
Although estranged and now divorced, the first person he wants to go to...
I think sometimes we get very skewed culturally.
The most daunting thing to go on a red carpet for me.
And I never butted heads with her after that.
I've seen, uh, girls in villages say that they have curbed child marriage because of sports.
And those were movies that had very closed off, and kind of aggressive men.

vocabulary.definition: Ability to make your own decisions and act on them without being forced.
vocabulary.example: She left the company of her own volition, not because she was fired.

vocabulary.definition: Treating a serious subject in a humorous or joking way.
vocabulary.example: Stop being facetious; this is a very serious meeting.

vocabulary.definition: No longer close or connected to someone you were once close to.
vocabulary.example: He hasn't spoken to his estranged father in years.

vocabulary.definition: Not balanced or fair; showing only one side of something.
vocabulary.example: The survey results were skewed because they only asked teenagers.

vocabulary.definition: Seeming difficult enough to make you feel nervous or less confident.
vocabulary.example: Moving to a new country can be a daunting prospect.

vocabulary.definition: Argued or disagreed strongly with someone.
vocabulary.example: The marketing manager and the sales director always butt heads over the budget.

vocabulary.definition: Kept under control or prevented from becoming worse.
vocabulary.example: You need to curb your spending if you want to save for a house.

vocabulary.definition: Unwilling to share feelings, thoughts, or personal experiences.
vocabulary.example: After the accident, he became very closed off to his friends.

culturalNotes.heading: "Of my own volition" vs. "My choice"
culturalNotes.subtext: "Volition" is highly formal and emphasizes independent decision-making, not just preference.

culturalNotes.heading: The Danger of Being "Facetious"
culturalNotes.subtext: Being called as funny is a compliment, but being called "facetious" is usually a criticism. It implies you are making jokes at the wrong time.

culturalNotes.heading: Visualizing "Butting Heads"
culturalNotes.subtext: This idiom comes from the literal image of two animals (like rams or goats) crashing their horns together. It perfectly shows two people refusing to back down.

commonMistakes.incorrect: He did it on his own violation.
commonMistakes.correct: He did it of his own volition.

commonMistakes.incorrect: Don't be so facetious, you're really funny!
commonMistakes.correct: Don't be so facetious — this is serious.
commonMistakes.explanation: "Facetious" is a criticism, not a compliment.

commonMistakes.incorrect: I butted head with my boss.
commonMistakes.correct: I butted heads with my boss.

commonMistakes.incorrect: The government curbed on illegal activities.
commonMistakes.explanation: "Curb" takes a direct object — no preposition needed.

quiz.question: Which word means treating a serious subject in a humorous or joking way?
She left the company of her own _______, not because she was fired.
Which word describes someone who is no longer close to a person they were once connected to?
The marketing manager and the sales director always _______ over the budget.
Which word means not balanced or fair, showing only one side of something?
Moving to a new country can be a _______ prospect.
Which phrase describes someone unwilling to share feelings or personal experiences?
You need to _______ your spending if you want to save for a house.
Which word means seeming difficult enough to make you feel nervous or less confident?
Stop being _______; this is a very serious meeting.
Which idiom means to argue or disagree strongly with someone?
The survey results were _______ because they only asked teenagers.
